metadata.title: Installing Splunk Add-on for Sysmon on Splunk Free Edition
metadata.author: Homelab Project
metadata.purpose: Set up proper ingestion and parsing of Sysmon logs from Windows Server 2025 into Splunk Free using the Universal Forwarder and the official Splunk Add-on for Sysmon (v5.0.0).

component.name: sysmon
component.value: Installed and running in Smart Mode (low noise configuration)

statusItem.description: Sysmon events were being collected via the Universal Forwarder.
Events arriving in Splunk showing as generic sourcetype (xmlwineventlog).
No proper field extractions: Image, CommandLine, ParentImage, hashes, etc.
Splunk Free UI blocked "Manage Apps" due to missing Auth license feature.

section.title: Download and Extraction Process
section.note: No .exe installer or additional archives are present. This is normal behaviour for Splunk add-ons — they are pure configuration files.

section.title: Installation Steps on Splunk Free

step.title: Copy Add-on to Splunk Apps Directory
step.description: Copy the entire extracted folder to the Splunk apps directory.

step.title: Restart Splunk
step.description: Restart Splunk using PowerShell (Run as Administrator) to load the new add-on.
step.note: Both PowerShell and Command Prompt work for this step.

step.title: Install on Universal Forwarder
step.description: Optionally install the same add-on on the Universal Forwarder for improved local parsing and field extraction at the source.

section.workaroundNote: Splunk Free edition blocks the web-based "Install app from file" because it requires the Auth license feature. Manual folder copy to etc\apps\ followed by a restart is the reliable workaround.

step.title: Confirm Add-on in Splunk Web
step.description: Open Splunk Web and confirm the add-on appears in the Apps list.
step.expectedResult: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Sysmon listed under Apps

step.title: Sourcetype Check Search
step.description: Run a search to verify the sourcetype has changed correctly.
step.splQuery: index=wineventlog source=*sysmon* | stats count by sourcetype source

step.title: Field Extraction Test
step.description: Verify that key Sysmon fields are being extracted properly.
step.splQuery: index=wineventlog sourcetype=*sysmon* | table _time host EventCode Image CommandLine ParentImage ProcessId hashes DestinationIp

lesson.finding: Splunk Free blocks web-based app installation
lesson.detail: The "Manage Apps → Install app from file" feature in Splunk Free is disabled because it requires the Auth license feature not included in the free tier.
lesson.resolution: Manual folder copy to etc\apps\ and Splunk restart.

lesson.finding: Two-step extraction is required for Splunk add-ons
lesson.detail: The download is a .tgz file which must be extracted twice: .tgz to .tar, then .tar to folder.
lesson.resolution: Use 7-Zip or Windows built-in Extract All for each layer.

lesson.detail: The renderXml = true setting in the Universal Forwarder's inputs.conf is essential. Without it, the Sysmon add-on cannot properly parse the incoming events.
lesson.resolution: Ensure inputs.conf on the Forwarder includes renderXml = true for the Sysmon input.

lesson.detail: Both PowerShell and Command Prompt work reliably for restarting Splunk and the Universal Forwarder.

task.title: Tune Sysmon Configuration
task.description: Review and refine the Sysmon config.xml to reduce event noise and stay within Splunk Free's 500 MB/day indexing limit.

task.title: Create Dedicated Sysmon Index
task.description: Create a dedicated index (e.g., sysmon) to separate Sysmon events from other Windows event log data for easier searching and retention management.

task.title: Build Sysmon Dashboards
task.description: Create basic dashboards in Splunk for key Sysmon event codes: Process Creation (1), Network Connections (3), Image Load (7), File Creation (11), DNS Query (22).

task.title: Explore Splunk Security Content
task.description: Investigate Splunk Security Content detections and analytics that leverage Sysmon data for threat detection use cases.
